title: andropogon lineatus
intro: Andropogon lineatus, also known as East Indian thyme or Red grass, is a species of grass native to tropical Asia and the Pacific Islands, belonging to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Andropogon lineatus include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The herbal preparations of Andropogon lineatus include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
